metrics_explanation.MSJ: Mean Squared Jerk (rad²/s⁵) — lower is smoother. Min-jerk is the theoretical minimum.
metrics_explanation.planning_time: Wall-clock time to compute path (s) — lower is faster. Min-jerk is near-instant.
metrics_explanation.execution_time: Time to traverse planned path in simulation (s) — lower means shorter/faster path.
metrics_explanation.POR: Path Optimality Ratio = d_path / d_optimal. 1.0 = matched A* optimal. Higher = more detour.
metrics_explanation.collision_rate: % of trials where arm hit an obstacle. 0% ideal. A* is 0% by construction.
metrics_explanation.success_rate: % of trials where planner found a path. 100% ideal.

baseline_framing.description: All 7 algorithms are evaluated between two baselines that bound the performance space.
baseline_framing.floor_baseline: min_jerk — obstacle-blind, mathematically smoothest, near-instant planning, HIGH collision rate.
baseline_framing.ceiling_baseline: A* — optimal collision-free path, provides d_optimal for POR, SLOW planning, 0% collision.
baseline_framing.pass_criteria: collision < min_jerk AND planning_time < A* AND success >= 80%

MSJ.note: higher ratio = rougher path (expected for collision-aware planners)

planning_time.note: algorithms are expected to be slower than min-jerk

collision_rate.note: A* is 0% by construction; closer to 0% = better
